special_notes: CRITICAL DOCUMENT FOR TESTING: This report uses a graphical bar/scale format instead of a standard table. report_section: HAEMATOLOGY (SERUM VITAMIN B12) + SEROLOGY (VITAMIN D 25 HYDROXY — CLIA)
report_date: REDACTED (Srl No. 119 — same patient as GT-029)

findings.notes: Within reference range — towards lower half of normal

Vitamin D deficiency (14.0 ng/ml). Deficiency is defined as <20 ng/ml; insufficiency as 20-29 ng/ml; sufficiency as ≥30 ng/ml. This patient is deficient. Expected value note printed: 'desirable concentration of vitamin D (25-OH) is ≥30 ng/ml (≥75 nmol/L)' per Vieth R et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2007;85:649-650.

special_notes: Same patient as GT-029 (Srl No. 119, Swastik Hospital). Page 8 — companion page to GT-029 page 3. Combined clinical picture from both pages: elevated ESR + elevated CRP + Vitamin D deficiency — consistent with chronic inflammatory condition and possible nutritional deficiency. Both reports signed by Dr. Suryoshm Sharma and Dr. Gunjan Nain. Printed reference interpretation text states Vitamin D is acquired via sunlight or food and metabolised in liver. Note: unlike GT-008 (Lupin Vitamin B12 graphical chart), this Vitamin B12 is a standard numeric table — easy for OCR. Good contrast pair with GT-008 to test graphical vs numeric B12 extraction.
